# Olum (olumjs)

> Olum is a small, Vue-like JavaScript framework where components are plain `.html` files with a
> `<script>` (logic) block, an optional scoped `<style>`, and a template using control tags
> (`<if>`, `<else-if>`, `<else>`, `<show>`, `<for>`, `<transition>`). A Node compiler turns each
> `.html` component into a JS module; a tiny browser runtime builds and diff-patches the DOM.
> Ships with a file-based router (`olum-router`) and a zustand-style global store (`olum-store`).
> Scope: prototypes, demos, hackathons, and small apps.

This file is a complete reference for generating correct Olum code. The single design principle:
**is an attribute fundamentally CODE or a STRING?** Native HTML already answers it — `onclick=""`
is code, `class=""` is a string. Code-shaped attributes (`when`, `each`, `key`, `on*`, `html`)
take a JS expression directly inside `""`. Everything else is a literal string with `{expr}` for
dynamic parts. **No naked braces, no colon-bindings, no directives to memorize.**

---

## Project structure

```
src/
├── page.html            → route "/"        (a folder is a route only if it has page.html)
├── not-found.html       → global 404 page  (must be at src/ root; lowercase)
├── main.js              → OPTIONAL entry. Omit it → router auto-generated from the file tree.
├── about/page.html      → "/about"
├── blog/page.html       → "/blog"
├── blog/[slug]/page.html→ "/blog/:slug"    (dynamic segment → params().slug)
├── users/[id]/posts/[postId]/page.html → "/users/:id/posts/:postId"
├── (marketing)/pricing/page.html → "/pricing"  (parenthesized group dropped from URL)
├── 01-guide/page.html   → "/guide"         (leading "NN-" prefix stripped; other digits kept: 7guis → /7guis)
├── _drafts/…            → NO route          (leading underscore = skipped entirely)
├── components/          → NO route          (reserved folder; PascalCase component files live here)
└── utils/               → NO route          (reserved folder; stores/helpers live here)
```

- Route files: `page.html`, `not-found.html` (exact lowercase, case-sensitive).
- Reusable components: `src/components/AddTodo.html` — PascalCase filenames.
- Static route beats dynamic route on conflict. No catch-all `[...slug]` — each `[param]` = exactly one segment.
- After each route mounts, router fires `window` event `viewLoaded` (useful for scroll restoration/analytics).

### Static assets
Files in `public/` are served at the site root. **Always reference with root-absolute URLs starting with `/`**
(templates AND `<style>`): `/assets/logo.svg`, never `assets/logo.svg` (relative resolves against the
current route and 404s in production). `public/index.html` is the app shell; `public/main.css` is the global stylesheet.

---

## Component file structure

One `.html` file, up to three parts: `<script>` (logic), `<style>` (scoped CSS), and the template (everything else).

```html title="Counter.html"
<script>
  const state = { count: 0 };
  const inc = () => state.count++;
</script>

<style>
  .counter { font-weight: 700; }
</style>

<div class="counter">
  Count: {state.count}
  <button onclick="inc()">+</button>
</div>
```

- The **filename is the component name** — `CounterCard.html` → `<CounterCard />`. Component tags are **PascalCase**; that's how the compiler distinguishes a component from a plain element.
- `<script>` and `<style>` are both optional. Markup-only files are valid static components.
- Every instance renders inside a runtime wrapper `<div>` — that wrapper is `host` (see Lifecycle).
- Import components by their file name (`.html` compiles to `.js`, so imports reference `.js`... but you write the source name):
  ```html
  <script>
    import Navbar from "./components/Navbar";
    import { formatDate } from "./utils";
  </script>
  ```

---

## Text interpolation

`{expr}` in text is evaluated as JS and **HTML-escaped by default** (XSS-safe).

```html
<p>Hello {state.user.name}</p>
<p>Total: {state.items.length} items</p>
<p>{state.count > 0 ? 'positive' : 'zero'}</p>
```

- `null` / `undefined` render as an empty string.
- No escape for a literal `{` in text — any `{…}` is interpolation. Use `{String.fromCharCode(123)}` or a const holding `"{"`.

---

## Attributes

**String attributes (default):** a literal string; use `{expr}` inside for dynamic parts.
```html
<div class="card {state.active ? 'is-active' : ''}"></div>
<a href="/users/{state.user.id}" title="Open {state.user.name}">Profile</a>
<div style="color:{state.color}; background:{state.bg}; padding:8px;">box</div>  <!-- inline style = string + {expr}, kebab-case CSS -->
```

**Code attributes (value is an expression):** `when`, `each`, `key`, `on*`, `html` — the `""` value is JS.
```html
<if when="state.count > 0"> … </if>
<button onclick="inc()">+</button>
```

**Boolean attributes (presence toggle):** when the whole value is a single `{expr}`, the attribute is emitted when truthy, omitted when falsy.
```html
<button disabled="{state.busy}">Save</button>
<input type="checkbox" checked="{state.on}" />
<details open="{state.expanded}">…</details>
```
Recognized: `checked, disabled, selected, readonly, required, hidden, autofocus, multiple, open, loop, muted, controls, autoplay, novalidate, default, defer, ismap, reversed`. Only applies when value is exactly one `{expr}`.

---

## Events

Native `on*` attributes hold **code** (like real HTML). Two forms:
```html
<button onclick="inc()">+</button>
<button onclick="inc(), log()">multi call</button>
<input oninput="(e) => state.text = e.target.value" />   <!-- inline arrow, auto-extracted -->
<button onclick="e => state.n++">no-paren arrow</button>
<input oninput="setValue($event)" />                      <!-- $event = the DOM event -->
```

- **No event modifiers.** Do it in the handler: `onsubmit="(e)=> { e.preventDefault(); save() }"`.
- **One `on*` attribute per element.** For a second listener, attach it imperatively in `onMount` (see below).
- Inside `<for>`, handlers can reference loop variables directly: `<button onclick="remove(item)">`.
- On a **component** tag, any `on*` name is a function prop, NOT a DOM event.

---

## Conditionals — `<if>` / `<else-if>` / `<else>`

`when` is a JS expression. The element is **added/removed** from the DOM.
```html
<if when="state.tab === 'a'"><p>Tab A</p></if>
<else-if when="state.tab === 'b'"><p>Tab B</p></else-if>
<else><p>Fallback</p></else>
```

## Show / Hide — `<show>`

Like `<if>`, but keeps content in the DOM and only toggles visibility (`display: contents` ↔ `none`).
Content nodes are never replaced, so a playing `<video>`, animation, or typed input survives toggling.
```html
<show when="state.visible"><div class="panel">…</div></show>
```
Don't wrap `<tr>`/`<option>` in `<show>` (its wrapper `<div>` is invalid there) — use `<if>` instead.

## Loops — `<for>`

`each` is a JS expression. Three forms:
```html
<for each="fruit of state.fruits"><li>{fruit.name}</li></for>       <!-- array: "item of arr" -->
<for each="i of 6"><span>Step {i}</span></for>                       <!-- numeric range: i goes 1→N (literal number ONLY) -->
<for each="key in state.settings"><div>{key}</div></for>             <!-- object keys: "key in obj" -->
```
Index + array params (like `Array.map`), wrap in parens:
```html
<for each="(cat, index, arr) of state.cats"><li>{index+1}/{arr.length}: {cat.name}</li></for>
<for each="(key, index, value) in state.settings"><div>{index}. {key} = {value}</div></for>  <!-- value = obj[key] -->
```
**Keyed loops — add `key`** (a JS expr, no braces, unique per item) so items are matched by identity across reorders/removals instead of by position. Needed to keep DOM state (checkboxes, videos, typed text) on the right row:
```html
<for each="todo of state.todos" key="todo.id"><TodoRow todo="{todo}" /></for>
<for each="todo of state.todos" key="todo.id"><li>{todo.text}</li></for>
```
Dynamic count range: `each="i of Array.from({ length: state.n }, (_, k) => k + 1)"` (a literal number is required for the `of N` form).

---

## State & Reactivity

Declare reactive state as a **literal top-level `const state = { ... }`**. Mutating `state` re-renders. Reactivity is **deep** (nested objects, arrays, `Map`, `Set`).

```html
<script>
  const state = { count: 0, user: { name: "Ann" } };
  const inc = () => state.count++;
  const rename = () => (state.user.name = "Bo");   // nested mutation re-renders too
</script>
<p>{state.count} — {state.user.name}</p>
```

- Only `state` is reactive. Plain `const`/`let` are not tracked.
- Deep mutations that re-render: `state.user.name = "Bo"`, `state.todos.push(t)`, `state.todos[0].done = true`, `state.tags.add("x")`.
- Non-plain objects (`Date`, DOM nodes, class instances) are NOT tracked — assign back to a top-level key to re-render.
- Re-assigning the **same reference** (`state.todos = state.todos`) is a no-op. Force a re-render with a fresh value (spread, `map`, `filter`, `slice`).
- `const state = makeState()` is NOT recognized — must be a literal object in the component.
- Re-renders **diff-patch the DOM in place** (untouched nodes survive: focus, caret, scroll, playing media). Re-renders are **microtask-batched** (many writes → one patch). In tests, `window.olum.flushUpdates()` settles pending re-renders synchronously.

### Derived values = plain functions (no "computed")
```html
<script>
  const state = { count: 1 };
  const doubled = () => state.count * 2;
  const quadrupled = () => doubled() * 2;   // derived can call derived
</script>
<p>{doubled()} {quadrupled()}</p>
```

### Watchers — `const watcher = { key(old, next) {} }`
Fires on **top-level key assignment** only (not nested mutations).
```html
<script>
  const state = { count: 0, log: "—" };
  const watcher = { count(old, next) { state.log = `${old} → ${next}`; } };
</script>
```
For a nested change to fire a watcher, assign a fresh value to the key: `state.user = { ...state.user, name: "Bo" }`.

---

## Components & Props

Use a component by its **PascalCase** tag; import it in `<script>`.
```html title="Parent.html"
<script>
  import StatusBadge from "./StatusBadge";
  const state = { score: 5, name: "Ann" };
</script>
<StatusBadge label="Online" color="#16a34a" />
<CounterCard title="Counter" count="{state.score}" data="{state.user}" greet="Hi {state.name}" />
```

**Prop value rules:**
- `title="Hello"` → string (literal).
- `count="{n + 1}"` → the expression's **real value/type** (number, object, etc.) when the whole value is one `{expr}`.
- `greet="Hi {state.name}"` → interpolated **string**.
- **No spread** (`<Comp {...obj} />`) and **no shorthand** (`<Comp {a} />` / `<Comp a={a} />`). Pass each field: `<Comp a="{obj.a}" />`.
- Prop names must be plain camelCase identifiers (dashes truncate the name on component tags).

**Reading props in the child** — `props()` imported from `olum`, called once at the **top level** of `<script>`:
```html title="StatusBadge.html"
<script>
  import { props } from "olum";
  const { label, color } = props();          // destructured names stay LIVE
  const { size = "md" } = props();            // default when parent omits it
  const { theme: t = "dark" } = props();      // alias + default
</script>
<span style="color:{color}">{label}</span>
```
Destructuring from `props()` is a **compiler feature**: each destructured name compiles into a fresh `props().name` read, so it stays current after the parent re-renders. `{props().label}` is equivalent.

**Stays live:** plain names, aliases, defaults, `children`, function props.
**One-time snapshot (NOT live):** `...rest`, nested patterns `{ user: { name } }`, computed keys `{ [key]: v }`, destructures not at the top level of `<script>`, indirect (`const p = props(); const { a } = p`), a default referencing another destructured prop.

**Props are read-only (one-way data flow).** Data flows down via props; changes flow up via **callback props**:
```html title="CounterCard.html"
<!-- parent owns the value + hands down a setter -->
<CounterCard value="{state.score}" onChange="{setScore}" />
<!-- child reads the prop, calls the callback to request a change -->
<script>
  import { props } from "olum";
  const { value, onChange } = props();
  const inc = () => onChange(value + 1);
</script>
```
Assigning to a destructured prop throws (`const`); never mutate props. On a **component** tag, `on*` names (even `onclick`) are just function props the child chooses when to call.

---

## Slots — `children`

Content between a component's tags is exposed as `children` on `props()` (injected as **raw trusted markup**, not escaped — don't route untrusted strings through a slot).
```html title="Box.html"
<script>
  import { props } from "olum";
  const { children } = props();   // live
</script>
<div class="box">
  <if when="children">{children}</if>
  <else><em>no content</em></else>   <!-- fallback when parent passed nothing -->
</div>
```

## Scope — public/private surface (advanced)

Top-level props and methods are **private by default**. Scope attributes on the `<script>` tag expose them:
`public`, `public-props`, `public-methods`, `private*` (default), plus `exclude-<kebab-name>` exceptions (read left-to-right; each keyword opens a group, following `exclude-*` attach to it).
```html
<script public-props exclude-age private-methods exclude-get-age>…</script>
```
Read a **mounted** component's public surface with `scope("Name"[, index])` from `olum` → `{ props, methods, state, el, key }` (or `null`). Escape hatch — prefer the store for shared state.

---

## Lifecycle — `onMount`

Import `onMount` from `olum`; the callback runs on mount; **return a cleanup function** that runs on unmount (e.g. an `<if>` toggles it off, or a keyed item is removed). Call `onMount` **once** per component.
```html
<script>
  import { onMount } from "olum";
  const state = { photos: [], seconds: 0 };

  onMount(async () => {
    const res = await fetch("/api/photos");   // async: the place to fetch initial data
    state.photos = await res.json();

    const id = setInterval(() => state.seconds++, 1000);
    window.addEventListener("keydown", onKey);
    const main = host.querySelector("main");  // `host` = this component's root element (no import)

    return () => {                             // cleanup: timers, listeners, actions
      clearInterval(id);
      window.removeEventListener("keydown", onKey);
    };
  });
</script>
```
`host` is the component's own root element — use it instead of `document.querySelector`. `onMount` is also where you do **refs**, **actions** (there is no `bind:this` / `use:action`), and **global `window`/`document` listeners** — all wired imperatively and cleaned up in the return.

---

## Two-way binding (manual — there is no `model`)

Every binding is a value-ish attribute reading state + an event handler writing back.
```html
<input value="{state.text}" oninput="(e) => state.text = e.target.value" />
<textarea value="{state.text}" oninput="(e) => state.text = e.target.value"></textarea>
<input type="number" value="{state.n}" oninput="(e) => state.n = +e.target.value" />  <!-- +cast: e.target.value is a string -->
<input type="checkbox" checked="{state.yes}" onchange="(e) => state.yes = e.target.checked" />
<input type="radio" name="s" checked="{state.scoops === 1}" onchange="setScoops(1)" />
<select onchange="(e) => state.color = e.target.value">
  <option value="red" selected="{state.color === 'red'}">red</option>   <!-- selected on OPTION -->
</select>
<input type="file" multiple onchange="(e) => state.files = e.target.files" />  <!-- FileList; Array.from() to loop -->
```
`value="{expr}"` on the `<select>` element itself silently does nothing — put `selected="{expr}"` on each option.
For `<select multiple>`: `Array.from(e.target.options).filter(o => o.selected).map(o => o.value)`.

---

## Raw HTML & Security

Everything interpolated is escaped by default (`olum.esc`). Opt out only for **trusted/sanitized** HTML:
```html
<div html="state.articleHtml"></div>        <!-- element-level: content becomes raw HTML of the expr -->
<p>Intro: {olum.html(state.snippetHtml)} — end.</p>   <!-- inline, per-value -->
```
Both are greppable opt-outs. **Never pass untrusted user content to `html=` or `olum.html()`** — sanitize first (e.g. DOMPurify).

---

## Scoped CSS

A component's `<style>` is auto-scoped (compiler tags elements with a unique attribute). Just write normal CSS.
```html
<style>
  .title { color: #4f46e5; }         /* scoped — won't leak */
  :root { --brand: #25c97e; }        /* :root / html / body selectors stay GLOBAL (escape hatch) */
</style>
```
- `@keyframes` are renamed per-component (no collisions); `@media/@supports/@layer/@container` inner rules are scoped; `@font-face` untouched.
- `url(...)` in component CSS must be root-absolute (`url(/assets/x.svg)`).
- No `:host` selector — style your template elements directly. App-wide styles go in `public/main.css`.

---

## Transitions — `<transition>`

Wrap **one element** to animate its enter/leave. Intro plays when it enters (`<if>` adds it); removal is deferred so the outro plays first.
```html
<if when="state.visible">
  <transition transition="fade"><p>Fades in and out</p></transition>   <!-- same in+out -->
</if>
<transition in="fly({ y: 200, duration: 2000 })" out="fade"><p>…</p></transition>  <!-- separate in/out + params -->
```
- Attributes: `transition="fn(params)"` (both), `in="fn(params)"`, `out="fn(params)"`. `params` is a normal object evaluated at render time (can read `state` / loop vars).
- **Built-ins** (no import): `fade`, `fly` (x,y,opacity), `scale` (start,opacity), `slide` (height), `draw` (SVG path). All take `delay`, `duration`, and `easing` (a **name string**, e.g. `'quintOut'`, `'bounceInOut'`; full set on `olum.easings`).
- **Custom:** a function `(node, params) => ({ delay?, duration?, easing?, css?(t,u), tick?(t,u) })` — same contract as Svelte. `t` 0→1 on enter, 1→0 on leave; `u = 1 - t`. Define in `<script>` and name it. Params are JSON-serialized (can't pass a function as a param).
- **Lifecycle events:** `onintrostart` / `onintroend` / `onoutrostart` / `onoutroend` = bare method names.
- **Keyed lists:** works on items inside a keyed `<for>` (each plays its in/out as items add/remove).
- **`flip`** attribute: animates keyed items to their new position on reorder (needs a keyed `<for>`). Takes `duration`, `delay`, `easing` (name).
- **`crossfade()`** from `olum` returns `[send, receive]`; put `out="send({ key })"` on the leaving element and `in="receive({ key })"` on the entering one with the same key to fly between two spots. Wrap as named consts (it's destructured). Key off a **primitive id**, not an object.

---

## Debugging — `<log>`

Compiles to `console.log(expr)` that runs on initial render + whenever the logged value changes; renders nothing.
```html
<log>{state.user}</log>
<log>{state.a, state.b}</log>   <!-- multiple args pass through -->
```

---

## Router (olum-router)

File-based (see Project structure). No route config — the file tree IS the route table. History mode by default (clean URLs); falls back to hash mode if the History API is missing.

**Links — `to` attribute** (client-side navigation, no reload):
```html
<a to="/about">About</a>          <!-- router intercepts; adds `active` class when URL matches -->
<a href="/about">Hard reload</a>  <!-- normal browser nav -->
```
Keep `to` anchor content **plain text** (a nested `<span>`/icon intercepts the click and the nav misses). Style the current route with `a.active { … }`. Clicking the current route is a no-op.

**Programmatic navigation** from `olum`:
```js
import { push, replace, back, forward, go, pathname } from "olum";
push("/cart");        // pushState — Back returns to prev page
replace("/dashboard"); // replaceState — for redirects (login), removes current from history
back(); forward(); go(-2);
pathname();           // current route path (normalized, leading slash, no trailing)
```

**Route params** — `params()` from `olum`, called once at top level of `<script>`:
```html title="src/blog/[slug]/page.html"
<script>
  import { params } from "olum";
  const { slug } = params();   // values arrive URL-decoded then LOWERCASED
</script>
<h1>blog: {slug}</h1>
```
Multiple dynamic segments work (`/users/[id]/posts/[postId]`). Static route wins over dynamic. No catch-all.

**404:** add `not-found.html` at the root of `src/`.

**Opting out / manual entry** — write `src/main.js` yourself and the compiler skips router generation:
```js title="src/main.js"
import Olum from "olum";
import page from "./page.js";
new Olum().$("#app").use(page);   // single component, no router (can drop olum-router from package.json)
```
Manual router config: build a `routes` array of `{ path, comp }`, `new Router({ mode: "history", root: "/", routes, err: "/404" })`, `new Olum().$("#app").use(router)`.

---

## Global Store (olum-store)

Zustand-style shared state: define once in a plain `.js` module, import anywhere; components **subscribe by reading** it during render and re-render on change. No providers, no wiring.
```js title="store.js"
import { store } from "olum";
export const cart = store({
  items: [],
  add(name) { this.items.push(name); },   // METHOD shorthand — `this` is the store
  clear() { this.items = []; },
  // clear: () => (this.items = []),       // ✗ arrow: `this` is NOT the store
});
```
```html title="Basket.html"
<script>
  import { cart } from "./store";
</script>
<p>{cart.items.length} items</p>          <!-- this read subscribes Basket to cart -->
<for each="name of cart.items"><li>{name}</li></for>
<button onclick="cart.clear()">Empty</button>
```
- Same **deep reactivity** as `state` (nested objects/arrays/Map/Set). Non-plain objects untracked. Same-reference/no-change writes are skipped.
- **Writes are microtask-batched** — many writes in a tick paint once. Component `state` batches the same way, so the store is **not** faster for large collections; choose it because the data is **shared**, not for performance. DOM isn't updated immediately after a write (`await Promise.resolve()` to read it).
- Mutating from outside an action works (`cart.items = []` in a handler). Split by concern (`cart.js`, `session.js`). `store(init)` takes a plain object (no class instances). `olum` auto-loads `olum-store`.

### state vs props vs store
- **state** — one component; microtask-batched re-render; dies with the component.
- **props** — parent → child; read-only; re-renders with the parent; dies with the child.
- **store** — whole app; microtask-batched; lives as long as the app runs.
- Rules: local first (state); one level down is props (+ callback prop to report up); shared or long-lived (survives route changes) is the store. **Prop-drilling through a component that doesn't use the prop = move it to a store.**

---

## Build & Deploy

`npm run build` compiles every component, bundles with vite, writes static output to `dist/`. Compile errors fail the build (non-zero exit). Everything in `public/` is included. Host `dist/` anywhere static, but configure an **SPA fallback** to `index.html` (history mode):
```
Netlify  public/_redirects:  /*  /index.html  200
Vercel   vercel.json:        { "rewrites": [{ "source": "/(.*)", "destination": "/index.html" }] }
```

---

## Common mistakes (✗ → ✓)

| ✗ Don't | ✓ Do | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `when={x}` / `each={x}` | `when="x"` / `each="x"` | code values live inside `""` |
| `value={state.x}` | `value="{state.x}"` | string attr + `{}` |
| `oninput={(e)=>…}` | `oninput="(e)=>…"` | events in `""` |
| `<Comp {a} />` / `<Comp a={a} />` | `<Comp a="{a}" />` | no shorthand / no naked-brace prop |
| `<Comp {...obj} />` | `<Comp a="{obj.a}" b="{obj.b}" />` | no spread — pass each field |
| `:style="{…}"` / `:title="x"` | `style="color:{x}"` / `title="{x}"` | string + `{}` |
| `model="state.x"` | `value="{state.x}"` + `oninput` | bind manually |
| `mode="prevent"` | `e.preventDefault()` in handler | no event modifiers |
| literal `{` in text | `{String.fromCharCode(123)}` | any `{…}` is interpolated |
| `<comp/>` | `<Comp/>` | components are PascalCase |
| `<select value="{x}">` | `selected="{x === …}"` on each `<option>` | value on select does nothing |
| `bind:this` / element refs | `host.querySelector(…)` in `onMount` | no refs |
| `use:action` | wire up in `onMount`, clean up in its return | no action directive |
| `each="i of state.n"` | `each="i of Array.from({length: state.n}, (_,k)=>k+1)"` | numeric range needs a literal number |
| two `on*` on one element | one handler; extra listeners via `onMount` | one `on*` per element |
| two `onMount(…)` calls | one call, one combined cleanup | only the first is wired |
| `params()` / `props()` twice | destructure once at top level | only the first call is compiled |
| `const { a, ...rest } = props()` | read extras via `props()` | `rest` is a snapshot, not live |
| `const { color } = props(); color = "x"` | `onChange("x")` via callback prop | props are read-only |
| `state.todos = state.todos` | assign a fresh array (spread/map/filter) | same-reference write is a no-op |
| `url(assets/x.svg)` / `src="assets/…"` | `/assets/…` (leading slash) | asset URLs are root-absolute |
| arrow action in store | method shorthand `add() {}` | arrows don't bind `this` |

---

## Known limitations

- **No AST compiler** (regex + jsdom) — robust for common cases, fragile at some edges.
- **Global reactivity bus** — broadcast dispatch, O(N) per update.
- **No element refs / actions / dynamic `<component is>` / dimension bindings** — all done imperatively in `onMount`.
- **No integrated component testing** framework yet.
- **No TypeScript** in components.
- Dynamic-component switching: use an `<if>/<else-if>/<else>` chain over a string in state.
